title: castilleja grisea
intro: Castilleja grisea, also known as Grey Indian Paintbrush or Mountain Paintbrush, is a perennial flowering plant native to the western United States and Canada. It belongs to the family Orobanchaceae and is a hemiparasitic plant, meaning it obtains some of its nutrients by parasitizing the roots of other plants. The medicinal properties of Castilleja grisea include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which have been reported to provide health benefits including reducing fever, soothing respiratory issues, and supporting immune function.
